start.text: Mr. Crane, the caretaker, polishes a spotless patch of the dusty front desk. 'Welcome to the Grand Palm Resort... or what's left of it. You kids shouldn't be here after dark. The Phantom doesn't like guests.'

choices.text: 'The ledger says supplies were billed to Room 217 — which doesn't exist.'

ghost.text: 'Glows green as swamp gas, wails like a busted pipe organ. It's scared off every buyer I've shown the property to. Some nights I see it drifting across the grounds toward the old boiler room...'

janitor.text: 'Gus? Harmless enough. Keeps to himself. Though he does spend an awful lot of time in that locked closet of his. Lost his key recently, or so he claims.'

sale.text: Crane's eye twitches. 'Between us? If the resort doesn't sell soon, the bank takes everything. Though with the hauntings, the price keeps dropping... and dropping...' He smiles faintly, then catches himself.

ledger_press.text: Crane goes very still. 'You read the ledger? Nosy little... ahem. A clerical error, nothing more. Room 217 was — water damaged. Yes. Don't go poking around the boiler room, it isn't SAFE.' He didn't mention the boiler room before you did.

start.text: Janitor Gus stops raking and eyes you warily over the dead leaves. 'Resort's closed, kids. Nothin' out here but weeds and bad memories.'

choices.text: Show him the can of glowing paint

ghost.text: 'Ain't no ghost.' He grips the rake tighter. 'Just drafts, bad wiring, and folks seein' what they're told to see. Now scram before you get hurt. Or... before somethin' hurts you.'

shed.text: 'Toolshed? Dropped my maintenance key in there weeks back. Too dark to find it, and the boss won't spring for a new bulb. If you fish it out, don't go usin' it on my closet, y'hear?'

confront.text: You hold up the can of LUMO-GLOW paint. Gus goes white as a — well, you know. 'Where'd you— okay, OKAY! I bought that paint, but only 'cause MR. CRANE told me to! Said it was for renovatin' the pool mural! Six cans of ghost-green paint for a mural, and him prowlin' the boiler room every night — I ain't stupid, kids, and I ain't takin' the fall!'

truth.text: Gus nods grimly. 'Catch him in the act and I'll back your story. He keeps his getup down in the boiler room, under my closet. There's an old laundry-net rig down there — Freddy-style trap practically builds itself.'

choices.text: 'Thanks, Gus. Gang — to the boiler room!'

start.text: You yank the lever! The Phantom Guest — mid-lunge at the Scooby Snack — is snatched into the laundry net and hauled kicking toward the ceiling! 'RELEASE ME, MEDDLERS! THE PHANTOM CURSES YOU ALLLL!'

reveal.text: Velma yanks off the glowing sheet... 'MR. CRANE?!' The caretaker sags in the net. 'That's right! With the resort 'haunted', the price kept dropping — until I could buy paradise myself for pennies! And I would have gotten away with it too, if it weren't for you meddling kids and that mangy mutt!' Scooby grins: 'Rangy rutt? ROOBY-ROOBY-ROO!'
